title: Tomato Chutney

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Green chilies – 2 (adjust to heat preference), slit
Kashmiri red chili powder – 1½ tsp (for color and mild heat)
Tamarind pulp – 1 tbsp (optional for tang depth)

instruction.title: Method
steps: Prepare the tomatoes: Blanch tomatoes in boiling water for 2–3 minutes until skins split. Remove, cool slightly, peel, and chop finely (this gives a smooth, rich chutney base).

Temper the spices: Heat oil in a heavy-bottom pan over medium heat. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add fenugreek seeds, dry red chilies, and curry leaves — fry for 5–7 seconds (avoid burning fenugreek; it turns bitter).
Build the flavor base: Add onions, sauté until soft and golden. Stir in ginger, garlic, and green chilies; cook until raw aroma disappears.

Cook the chutney: Add turmeric powder and Kashmiri chili powder, stir briefly. Add blanched tomatoes, salt, and cook on medium heat until they soften and release oil (10–12 minutes). Add jaggery and tamarind pulp, stir well, and cook 3–4 more minutes until chutney thickens to a glossy consistency.
Finishing: Taste and balance salt, sweetness, and tang. Cool completely before storing in sterilized jars.

chefTips: Oil separation is the key sign your chutney is ready — it means the spices are fully cooked and shelf-stable.
Fenugreek caution: Too much will overpower the chutney; toast lightly for balance.
Storage: Refrigerate up to 2 weeks, or store in sterilized airtight jars for up to 3 months if oil layer covers the top.
Serving: Pairs beautifully with dosa, idli, chapati, pakora, or as a tangy sandwich spread.
